topic: An athlete is asked to switch positions
raw_output:
[story 1]

The roar of the crowd seemed to vibrate directly against my ribs, a physical pressure that made every breath shallow. Coach’s voice, sharp and final, sliced through the tension. "You're switching. Center to wing." My stomach plummeted. I stared at the polished floor, the familiar geometry of the court suddenly feeling alien and mocking. My hands, usually so sure, felt clumsy, heavy. Every glance from my teammates was a weight pressing down, cataloging the awkwardness of my forced transition. I mumbled a barely audible 'yes,' the sound catching in my throat like gravel. The walk to the new spot felt like wading through thick mud; every step was an announcement of my inadequacy.

[story 2]

"Just try it," the manager had said, offering a tight, encouraging smile that didn't reach his eyes. I could feel the heat rising up my neck, a flush spreading across my cheeks that I desperately wished I could extinguish. When the official announced the positional change, the air went thin. My jaw tightened until my molars ached. I looked over at my former spot, the one I had mastered, and felt a strange detachment, as if watching a stranger perform a dance I no longer understood. When I finally moved to the periphery, the movement was stiff, unnatural. I kept my eyes fixed on the ground, focusing intently on the scuff marks, desperate to make myself invisible in this new, unwelcome configuration.

[story 3]

The silence in the locker room after the announcement was deafening, punctuated only by the nervous shifting of gear. I could feel the scrutiny—the subtle shifts in posture, the way eyes lingered a fraction too long on my unfamiliar stance. My palms were slick. When the coach pointed to the new area, a cold knot formed in my chest. I tried to meet his gaze, but my eyes darted everywhere else, unable to sustain the direct connection. The physical discomfort of being out of my element was overwhelming; my muscles felt disconnected, unresponsive to the commands I was trying to issue. I just wanted the floor to swallow me whole, a private, dark space where this performance wasn't happening.
